title: Undercarriage with carrier plate for compensating for track link rail scalloped surface
abstract: An undercarriage (300) configured to support a track chain (204) and to be attached to a machine (100) with an endless track drive includes a first carrier plate (308) attached to the undercarriage (300), the first carrier plate (308) including a material having a hardness of at least 45 Rockwell Scale C or greater.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. An undercarriage (300) configured to support a track chain (204) and to be attached to a machine (100) with an endless track drive, the undercarriage (300) comprising: a plurality of bottom support rollers (212) rotatably attached to the undercarriage (300) including a front bottom support roller (212a) defining a front bottom support roller rotational axis (2l2aA) and a rear bottom support roller (2l2b) defining a rear bottom support roller rotational axis (2l2bA), a front wheel (302) rotatably attached to the undercarriage (300), defining a front wheel rotational axis (302A); a rear wheel (304) rotatably attached to the undercarriage, defining a rear wheel rotational axis (304 A); a track chain (204) including sets of links (216), each set of links (216) being joined to each other in a rotatable manner, at least one of the links (306) defining a link pitch distance (222) equivalent to the overall length of the at least one link (306); and a first carrier plate (308) attached to the undercarriage (300), the first carrier plate (308) including a material having a hardness of at least 45 Rockwell Scale C or greater. 2. The undercarriage (300) of claim 1 wherein the hardness ranges from 45 Rockwell Scale C to 60 Rockwell Scale C. 3. The undercarriage (300) of claim 2 wherein the undercarriage (300) defines a track chain traveling direction (310), the first carrier plate (308) defines a first carrier plate thickness (308T) in a direction perpendicular to the track chain traveling direction (310), and at least one of the links (306) defines a rail thickness (306T) in a direction perpendicular to the track chain traveling direction (310), and the ratio of the rail thickness (306T) to the carrier plate thickness (308T) ranges from .5 to 4.0. 4. The undercarriage (300) of claim 3 wherein the first carrier plate (308) defines a length (308L) measured along the track traveling chain direction (310) and a ratio of the length to the link pitch distance (222) ranges from 1.0 to 3.0. 5. The undercarriage (300) of claim 1 wherein the first carrier plate (308) comprises AR400 steel. 6. The undercarriage (300) of claim 3 further comprising an elevated drive sprocket (206) and the first carrier plate (308) is disposed along the track chain traveling direction (310) between the elevated drive sprocket (206) and the front wheel (302). 7. The undercarriage (300) of claim 6 further comprising a second carrier plate (312) that is disposed along the track chain traveling direction (310) between the elevated drive sprocket (206) and the rear wheel (304). 8. An undercarriage (300) configured to support a track chain (204) and to be attached to a machine (100) with an endless track drive, the undercarriage (300) comprising: a plurality of bottom support rollers (212) rotatably attached to the undercarriage (300) including a front bottom support roller (212a) defining a front bottom support roller rotational axis (2l2aA) and a rear bottom support roller (2l2b) defining a rear bottom support roller rotational axis (2l2bA); a front wheel (302) rotatably attached to the undercarriage (300), defining a front wheel rotational axis (302A); a rear wheel (304) rotatably attached to the undercarriage (300), defining a rear wheel rotational axis (304 A); a track chain (204) including sets of links (216), each set of links (216) being joined to each in a rotatable manner, at least one of the links (306) 

 defming a link pitch distance (222) equivalent to the overall length of the at least one link (216); and wherein the undercarriage (300) defines a track chain traveling direction (310), the first carrier plate (308) defines a first carrier plate thickness (308T) in a direction perpendicular to the track chain traveling direction (310), and at least one of the links (306) defines a rail thickness (306T) in a direction perpendicular to the track chain traveling direction (310), and the ratio of the rail thickness (306T) to the first carrier plate thickness (308T) ranges from .5 to 4.0. 9. The undercarriage (300) of claim 8 wherein the first carrier plate (308) has a hardness ranging from 45 Rockwell Scale C to 60 Rockwell Scale C. 10. The undercarriage (300) of claim 9 further comprising a second carrier plate (312) having a hardness ranging from 45 Rockwell Scale C to 60 Rockwell Scale C.
